perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN whether or not patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN only if patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction. Initially, all inventions become FRANKLIN's property regardless of filing. The modified text states that inventions become FRANKLIN's property *only* if filings occur. This creates uncertainty: are inventions without filings still FRANKLIN's? This contradicts the original clear assignment of all inventions. This creates an in-text contradiction with the rest of the section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall promptly and fully disclose to FRANKLIN any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements made by him or her pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN during his or her period of employment by FRANKLIN, and any improvements to his or her invention, writings, programs, and discoveries, made, conceived or acquired by him or her no later than one year after the termination of employment, whether made or conceived solely or jointly with others, whether during regular business hours or otherwise; said Inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements shall become and remain the property of FRANKLIN only if patent applications or copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed thereon.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that all inventions become FRANKLIN's property regardless of whether patent applications, copyright, trademark, or maskwork registrations are filed. The changed text modifies this to state that inventions become FRANKLIN's property *only* if such filings occur. This directly contradicts the original unconditional assignment of all inventions, creating a strong in-text contradiction within the document, as per the explanation provided. Additionally, the Contradicted Text is the Changed Text, which means the same text is contradicting itself.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, without further consideration.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, with further consideration to be negotiated upon each request.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states the employee will assign inventions to FRANKLIN "without further consideration." The modified clause says assignment requires "further consideration to be negotiated upon each request." This introduces a clear contradiction, creating uncertainty and potential disputes over compensation for assigning inventions. This change directly opposes the initial agreement, thus creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request and at the expense of FRANKLIN, make application through the attorneys for FRANKLIN for any letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations of the United States, and any and all foreign countries, on said inventions, discoveries, writings, programs or improvements, and assign and transfer all said applications, inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements to FRANKLIN or its nominee, with further consideration to be negotiated upon each request.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the employee will assign inventions without further consideration, while the changed text states that the assignment requires further consideration to be negotiated upon each request. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case, but the perturbation explanation explains that the change introduces a contradiction because the modified clause directly opposes the initial agreement by introducing a need for 'further consideration' where there was none before.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, upon request of FRANKLIN execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, only upon request of the employee's personal attorney, execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.explanation: Originally, the employee was required to execute documents upon FRANKLIN's request. Now, the employee is required to do so only upon request of the employee's personal attorney. This contradicts the initial obligation, giving the employee more control and potentially hindering FRANKLIN's ability to protect its intellectual property. The in-text contradiction lies in the shift of power dynamic in this section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE shall from time to time, only upon request of the employee's personal attorney, execute all papers and do all other things that may be reasonably required in order to protect the rights of FRANKLIN, and to vest in FRANKLIN or its successors or assigns the entire rights, title and interest in and to any and all inventions, discoveries, writings, program improvements, and applications for letters patent or copyright or maskwork registrations relating to anything pertaining to or useful in the business of FRANKLIN as provided above.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the employee shall execute papers only upon request of the employee's personal attorney. The contradicted text is exactly the same. The original text, however, states the employee should execute papers upon request of FRANKLIN. Therefore, the change introduces a direct contradiction with the original agreement, where FRANKLIN could request the employee to execute papers. Now, the execution of papers is solely based on the employee's attorney's request, hindering FRANKLIN's ability to protect its rights, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence until such time that such information has become widely known to the public or described in an issued patent or in a printed publication of wide circulation.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence for a period of one year after termination of employment.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies that confidentiality lasts until the information becomes public. The modified clause limits it to one year after termination. This introduces a contradiction in terms of duration. Confidential information might still be confidential after a year, but the employee is no longer obligated to protect it under this agreement. This contradiction is in-text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE further agrees not to divulge to any third party, either during his or her employment or thereafter, any confidential information conceived by him or her, disclosed by FRANKLIN, or obtained by him or her while in the employment of FRANKLIN relating in any way to any of FRANKLIN'S processes, businesses, customers, trade secrets, apparatus, products, software, packages, programs or trends in research, or to any of the inventions, discoveries, writings, programs, and improvements covered hereby, and agrees to maintain this information in confidence for a period of one year after termination of employment.
perturbation.justification: The original clause states that confidential information must be kept confidential until it becomes public knowledge. The changed clause limits this obligation to one year after termination, regardless of whether the information is still confidential. This creates a direct contradiction as the employee is no longer obligated to protect the information after one year, even if it remains confidential, which conflicts with the contradicted section's original intent and duration of confidentiality.

perturbation.original_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period is not made obligatory upon either party or a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period of one year is obligatory upon both parties as a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The initial agreement states that there is no obligation for continued employment for a definite period. This is directly contradicted by the modification, which enforces a one-year commitment as a condition of the agreement. This will cause an in-text contradiction as the contract now has contradicting statements.
perturbation.contradicted_text: EMPLOYEE understands and agrees that the continuance of EMPLOYEE in the service of FRANKLIN for a definite period of one year is obligatory upon both parties as a condition of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that there is no obligation for continued employment for a definite period. The changed text introduces an obligation for a definite period of one year. This directly contradicts the original statement, creating an in-text contradiction because now the contract contains conflicting statements about the employee's obligation for a one-year term. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which is permitted. The explanation given highlights how the Change causes a contradiction to the contradicted text.
